finding: UTS #39 restriction levels are a detection result over profile eligibility and augmented script sets; they must not be collapsed into syntax validity or treated as proof of benign intent.
design_implication: Expose restrictionLevel as an independent decision field after identifier-profile evaluation. Feed it authenticated exact-version Script and Script_Extensions data, and keep whole-script confusables, bidi safety, display policy, and intent as separate gates.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The classifier consumes pre-resolved augmented script sets and does not yet parse authenticated Unicode Script or Script_Extensions data files.
Recommended-script membership is injected and must be bound to the same Unicode version before production use.
The full Unicode 17.0.0 conformance corpus has not been executed.
falsification_route: Show disagreement with UTS #39 Section 5.2 for correct augmented script-set inputs.
Show a Greek or Cyrillic companion to Latin classified as Moderately Restrictive.
Show an outside-profile identifier classified at Levels 1-5.
Show a cardinality mismatch or invalid script code accepted silently.
privacy: No private chat, health, credential, or identifying material used or retained.
